# OpenClaw Console

> A native iOS + Android mobile app for supervising OpenClaw agents — the quiet mobile cockpit for developers and operators.

## Overview

OpenClaw Console is a dual-platform mobile application for monitoring and controlling OpenClaw AI agents. It connects directly to an OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket, providing a focused, distraction-free interface for agent supervision, CI/deploy monitoring, incident alerts, and biometric approvals.

## Vision

Be the definitive mobile cockpit for developers and operators who run OpenClaw, replacing noisy social channels with a purpose-built, secure work console.

## Use Cases

- Monitor OpenClaw agent task streams from mobile
- Approve dangerous actions (deploys, shell commands) with biometric confirmation
- View CI/CD pipeline status and PR dashboards
- Receive incident alerts and trigger runbooks
- Switch between multiple agents and workspaces

## Technical Stack

- **iOS**: Swift 6, SwiftUI, @Observable MVVM, LocalAuthentication (Face ID / Touch ID)
- **Android**: Kotlin 2.x, Jetpack Compose, Material Design 3, Hilt DI, BiometricPrompt
- **Skills Gateway**: TypeScript, WebSocket server on port 18789
- **Shared patterns**: Dark UI, task-first UX, gateway-first architecture

## Key Features

- Gateway connection + token auth (QR scan or manual entry)
- Agent & workspace switcher
- Task-first feed (grouped by job, not endless chat)
- Biometric approval flows for dangerous actions
- CI/PR dashboards (status, checks, labels)
- Incident view with severity and runbook triggers
- Configurable notifications (silent, warnings-only, critical-only)

## Repository Structure

- `ios/OpenClawConsole/` — Swift/SwiftUI iOS app
- `android/` — Kotlin/Compose Android app
- `openclaw-skills/` — TypeScript skills gateway
- `docs/` — Architecture, protocol, setup guides
- `.maestro/` — E2E test flows
- `marketing/` — Growth data and keyword strategy

## Building

### iOS
```
cd ios/OpenClawConsole && xcodebuild -scheme OpenClawConsole build
```

### Android
```
cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug
```

### Skills Gateway
```
cd openclaw-skills && npm ci && npm start
```

## Architecture & Protocols

### Communication Protocol
- **WebSocket**: WSS connection to gateway on port 18789 (TLS required in production)
- **Message Format**: JSON with `type`, `payload`, and optional `requestId`
- **Authentication**: Bearer token via Authorization header or `auth` message
- **Heartbeat**: Ping/pong keepalive every 30 seconds

### Security Requirements
- **Biometric Authentication**: All approval flows MUST require Face ID/Touch ID/Fingerprint
- **Certificate Pinning**: Production builds pin gateway TLS certificates
- **Encrypted Storage**: Android uses EncryptedSharedPreferences, iOS uses Keychain
- **No Bypass Modes**: Zero hardcoded approval bypasses in production code

### Development Guidelines
- **Git Flow**: Feature branches off `develop`, release branches to `main`
- **Atomic Commits**: One logical change per commit, conventional format
- **Architecture Bans**: No AndroidViewModel, collectAsState(), Handler(Looper), force casts without justification
- **Pre-commit Hooks**: Secret scanning, architecture compliance, linting enforced

### Testing Strategy
- **Unit Tests**: ViewModels, business logic, protocol parsing (100% coverage)
- **Integration Tests**: Gateway connections, authentication flows
- **E2E Tests**: Maestro flows for critical user journeys (launch, connect, approve)
- **Device Tests**: CI runs on Android emulator + iOS simulator

### Business Metrics
- **North Star**: Daily Active Approvers (DAA) - users approving ≥1 action/day
- **Revenue Goal**: $100/day via $10-20/month Pro subscriptions
- **Target Market**: Solo developers, DevOps, operators running self-hosted OpenClaw
- **Value Proposition**: Escape social messaging chaos, native mobile control plane

## File Structure Reference

```
openclaw-console/
├── android/                    # Android app (Kotlin/Compose)
│   ├── app/src/main/java/      # Main source code
│   └── app/build.gradle.kts    # Build configuration
├── ios/OpenClawConsole/        # iOS app (Swift/SwiftUI)
│   ├── OpenClawConsole/        # App source code
│   └── OpenClawConsole.xcodeproj/ # Xcode project
├── openclaw-skills/            # TypeScript skills gateway
│   ├── src/                    # Gateway source code
│   ├── src/skills/             # Individual skill modules
│   └── package.json            # Node.js dependencies
├── docs/                       # Architecture documentation
│   ├── architecture.md         # System design overview
│   ├── protocol.md            # WebSocket protocol spec
│   └── openclaw-setup.md      # Gateway setup guide
├── .maestro/                   # E2E test flows
│   ├── smoke-test-android.yaml # Android smoke test
│   └── smoke-test-ios.yaml     # iOS smoke test
├── .github/workflows/          # CI/CD pipelines
│   ├── ci.yml                 # Main CI (builds, tests, linting)
│   ├── device-tests.yml       # Emulator/simulator tests
│   └── native-release.yml     # App store releases
├── scripts/                    # Automation scripts
│   ├── pre-commit             # Git pre-commit hooks
│   └── preflight-release.sh   # Pre-release validation
└── marketing/                  # Growth and ASO data
    ├── keywords/               # App store keywords
    └── analytics/              # Growth metrics
```

## Development Commands

### Complete Build Verification
```bash
make verify                     # Run all tests and builds
```

### Platform-Specific Development
```bash
# Android
cd android && ./gradlew testDebugUnitTest assembleDebug
make run-android-emulator       # Launch on emulator

# iOS
cd ios/OpenClawConsole && xcodebuild -scheme OpenClawConsole test
make run-ios-sim               # Launch on simulator

# Skills Gateway
cd openclaw-skills && npm ci && npm run dev  # Starts on localhost:18789
```

### Release Preparation
```bash
./scripts/preflight-release.sh --platform both --layer 2
```
